# Project Charter — <project name>

PMBOK Initiating artifact. One document per project. Owned by
`project-manager`; authorized by the customer (recorded in
`CUSTOMER_NOTES.md`). Amend via a change log row in
`docs/pm/CHANGES.md`.

## 1. Purpose and justification

Why this project exists, the business or technical need it serves, and
what happens if it is not done.

## 2. Measurable objectives and success criteria

Each objective is specific, measurable, and time-bound. Map each
success criterion to at least one acceptance test or review gate.

| ID | Objective | Success criterion | Verified by |
|---|---|---|---|
| O-1 | | | |

## 3. High-level requirements

Two-paragraph summary of what is in scope. Detailed requirements live
in `docs/requirements.md` (shaped by
`docs/templates/requirements-template.md`).

## 4. High-level risks

Top 3–5 risks with short descriptions. Detailed register in
`docs/pm/RISKS.md`.

## 5. Summary milestones

| Milestone | Target date | Exit criterion |
|---|---|---|

## 6. Summary budget (time or cost)

Order-of-magnitude estimate and basis. Detailed baseline in
`docs/pm/COST.md`.

## 7. Stakeholders (summary)

Top stakeholders. Full register in `docs/pm/STAKEHOLDERS.md`.

## 8. Project manager and authority

Named `project-manager` teammate (per `docs/AGENT_NAMES.md`) and the
scope of decisions they may make without escalating. Escalation path
via `tech-lead`.

## 9. Sponsor

Customer name + the specific sign-off recorded in `CUSTOMER_NOTES.md`
that authorizes this charter.

## 10. Assumptions and constraints

Explicitly list — unstated assumptions are a risk category by
themselves.
